title: Interpret Tote Status Guidance From the Hospital HMI Operations Menu
summary: Use the Hospital HMI Operations Menu read-only status information to understand why a tote is at the station and identify the menu and action indicated by the WCS status message.
when_to_use: Use this reference when a station operator needs to read tote-related information on the Hospital HMI Operations Menu and determine, from the WCS-provided status message, why a tote is present at the induct or extract side and what menu and action are indicated.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to edit or change Induct Position or Extract Position information, because the source states this information is read-only and received from the WCS.
Do not use this procedure as a complete follow-on action procedure for specific tote exceptions, because this section does not provide the detailed actions for each status message.

responsibilities: View the Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen.
Identify whether the tote information is shown in the Induct Position area or Extract Position area.
Read AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status fields.
Use the WCS status message to understand why the tote is at the station and what menu and action are indicated.
Do not modify read-only position information.

safety_notes: Induct Position and Extract Position information on this screen is read-only and received from the WCS.
Do not edit or attempt to change the displayed Induct Position or Extract Position information.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen
Visibility of the Induct Position and Extract Position fields
WCS-provided status message displayed on the screen

access_required: Access to the Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen
system_preconditions: Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen is available for viewing.
Induct Position or Extract Position information is displayed on the screen.
A WCS-provided status message is present for the tote being reviewed.

visual_references.description: Hospital HMI Operations screen showing read-only Induct Position and Extract Position information, including AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.

steps.title: View the Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen
steps.purpose: Access the read-only tote status information presented on the Operations Menu.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen and locate the Induct Position and Extract Position areas.
steps.expected_result: The Operations Menu screen is visible and the operator can see the tote information areas for both station sides.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen shows Induct Position and Extract Position information fields.
steps.failure_condition: The Operations Menu screen is not visible or the position information areas cannot be viewed.

actions.label: View Operations Menu
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Figure 4-30 showing the Hospital HMI Operations Screen with Induct Position and Extract Position areas.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen cannot be accessed or viewed.
The Induct Position and Extract Position areas are not visible on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Induct Position" and "Extract Position" information on this screen is read-only and received from the WCS. The status messages will tell the station operator why the tote is there and what menu they need to use and what action they need to perform.

steps.title: Identify the relevant station side
steps.purpose: Determine whether the tote information to review is on the induct side or extract side of the station.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the tote information to review is in the Induct Position area or the Extract Position area.
steps.expected_result: The operator knows which side of the station contains the tote information to interpret.
steps.healthy_condition: One relevant position area is selected for review based on where the tote information is displayed.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot determine whether the tote information belongs to the Induct Position or Extract Position area.

actions.label: Select position area
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the tote information to review is in the Induct Position area or the Extract Position area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The separate Induct Position and Extract Position areas on the Operations Menu screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: It is unclear which side contains the tote information to be reviewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Induct Position" and "Extract Position" information on this screen is read-only and received from the WCS.
Induct Position and Extract Position fields include AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status. Status is a status message received from the WCS about the tote.

steps.title: Read the AGV number
steps.purpose: Identify the AGV associated with the selected side of the station.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed AGV# field for the selected side to identify the AGV at that side of the station.
steps.expected_result: The AGV number for the selected side is identified from the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: An AGV# value is visible and can be read from the selected position area.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV# field is missing, unreadable, or does not provide usable identification.

actions.label: Read AGV field
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed AGV# field for the selected side to identify the AGV at that side of the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The AGV# field within the selected Induct Position or Extract Position area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV# field is not visible or cannot be read.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Induct Position and Extract Position fields include AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.

steps.title: Read the tote ID
steps.purpose: Identify the tote currently associated with the selected side of the station.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed Tote ID field for the selected side to identify the tote at that side of the station.
steps.expected_result: The tote ID for the selected side is identified from the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: A Tote ID value is visible and can be read from the selected position area.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote ID field is missing, unreadable, or does not provide usable identification.

actions.label: Read Tote ID field
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed Tote ID field for the selected side to identify the tote at that side of the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote ID field within the selected Induct Position or Extract Position area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Tote ID field is not visible or cannot be read.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Induct Position and Extract Position fields include AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.

steps.title: Read the bar code field
steps.purpose: Capture the bar code information shown for the tote on the selected side.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed Bar Code field for the selected side to capture the information received from the tote bar code.
steps.expected_result: The bar code information for the selected side is identified from the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: A Bar Code value is visible and can be read from the selected position area.
steps.failure_condition: The Bar Code field is missing, unreadable, or does not provide usable information.

actions.label: Read Bar Code field
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed Bar Code field for the selected side to capture the information received from the tote bar code.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Bar Code field within the selected Induct Position or Extract Position area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Bar Code field is not visible or cannot be read.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Induct Position and Extract Position fields include AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.

steps.title: Interpret the status message
steps.purpose: Use the WCS-provided status message to understand why the tote is at the station and what menu and action are indicated.
steps.instruction: Read the Status field for the selected side and use the displayed WCS status message to determine why the tote is there and what menu and action the station operator needs to use or perform.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands the tote's purpose at the station and the menu/action indicated by the status message.
steps.healthy_condition: The Status field contains a WCS-provided message that clearly indicates why the tote is there and what menu or action is needed.
steps.failure_condition: The Status field is missing, unreadable, or does not clearly indicate the needed menu or action.

actions.label: Read Status field
actions.exact_action: Read the Status field for the selected side and use the displayed WCS status message to determine why the tote is there and what menu and action the station operator needs to use or perform.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Status field in the selected position area, which contains the WCS-provided tote status message.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed status message does not clearly indicate the needed menu or action.
The Status field is not visible or cannot be read.
The source section does not provide the detailed follow-on actions for the displayed status message.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The status messages will tell the station operator why the tote is there and what menu they need to use and what action they need to perform.
Status: A status message received from the WCS about the tote.

steps.title: Treat position information as read-only
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported changes to WCS-provided tote information on the Operations Menu.
steps.instruction: Treat the Induct Position and Extract Position information as read-only information received from the WCS.
steps.expected_result: The operator uses the displayed information for interpretation only and does not attempt to modify it.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator reads the information without attempting to edit or change any Induct Position or Extract Position fields.
steps.failure_condition: An attempt is made to edit or change read-only WCS-provided position information.

actions.label: Observe read-only information
actions.exact_action: Treat the Induct Position and Extract Position information as read-only information received from the WCS.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Induct Position and Extract Position information areas described as read-only and received from the WCS.

stop_or_escalate_if: There is any need to change the displayed position information.
A user is attempting to edit read-only WCS-provided information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Induct Position" and "Extract Position" information on this screen is read-only and received from the WCS.

success_criteria: The operator can identify whether the relevant tote information is in the Induct Position or Extract Position area.
The operator can read the AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status fields for the selected side.
The operator can use the WCS status message to understand why the tote is at the station and what menu and action are indicated.
The operator does not attempt to modify read-only WCS-provided position information.
healthy_conditions: The Hospital HMI Operations Menu screen is visible.
Induct Position and Extract Position fields are displayed.
AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status fields are readable.
The Status field provides a WCS-provided message that explains the tote context and indicates the needed menu or action.
The operator treats the displayed position information as read-only.
failure_conditions: The Operations Menu screen cannot be viewed.
The operator cannot determine whether the tote information is in the Induct Position or Extract Position area.
AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, or Status fields are missing or unreadable.
The displayed status message does not clearly indicate the needed menu or action.
An attempt is made to edit or change read-only Induct Position or Extract Position information.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or seek additional guidance if the displayed status message does not clearly indicate the needed menu or action.

Escalate if the required follow-on action is not described in this source section, because the source states the status message indicates the menu and action but does not provide the detailed follow-on steps here.
Escalate if the Operations Menu screen or required fields cannot be viewed.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The "Induct Position" and "Extract Position" information on this screen is read-only and received from the WCS. The status messages will tell the station operator why the tote is there and what menu they need to use and what action they need to perform.
Induct Position and Extract Position fields include AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status. Status is a status message received from the WCS about the tote.

missing_details: The source does not provide detailed follow-on procedures for each possible status message in this section.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify supporting roles beyond the station operator.
The source does not state whether production stop or LOTO is required.
The source does not provide commands or system navigation steps for opening the Operations Menu screen in this section.
